mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: The Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that continuously scans your Kubernetes cluster for security issues, providing automated vulnerability and compliance reports. It is ideal for organizations looking to enhance their cluster security posture through automated scanning and reporting.

steps.title: Install Trivy Operator using Helm
steps.description:
Add the Aqua Security Helm repository and install the Trivy Operator. Ensure you are using Helm version 3.0 or higher.

```bash
helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --version 0.23.0 --namespace trivy --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Trivy Operator is running successfully in the cluster.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n trivy
kubectl get deployment trivy-operator -n trivy
```

steps.title: Configure RBAC for Trivy Operator
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow the Trivy Operator to access necessary resources.

```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: trivy
  name: trivy-operator-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["*"]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log", "secrets", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]

---

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: trivy-operator-rolebinding
  namespace: trivy
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: trivy-operator-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: trivy-operator
  namespace: trivy
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
Remove the Trivy Operator and its associated resources from the cluster.

```bash
helm uninstall trivy-operator -n trivy
kubectl delete namespace trivy
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Trivy Operator, first backup existing configurations and reports. Then, run the Helm upgrade command.

```bash
helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --version 0.23.0 -n trivy
```

**Backup:** Ensure you back up any custom resources and configurations before upgrading. You can export them using:

```bash
kubectl get trivyreports --all-namespaces -o yaml > trivyreports-backup.yaml
```

**Rollback:** If issues arise, you can rollback to the previous version using:

```bash
helm rollback trivy-operator -n trivy
```

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator not scanning new Pods
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the Trivy Operator has the necessary permissions and that the deployment is running. Check logs for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app=trivy-operator -n trivy
```

troubleshooting.title: Failed to generate reports
troubleshooting.description: Check if the Trivy Operator has access to the required resources. Verify RBAC settings and ensure the necessary permissions are granted.

troubleshooting.title: Helm installation fails
troubleshooting.description: Ensure you are using a compatible version of Helm (3.0+). Check for network issues or repository access problems.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced enhanced compliance reporting features and improved performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vulnerability scanning capabilities and improved RBAC configurations.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of the Trivy Operator will have the operator running in the specified namespace, with the ability to scan Kubernetes resources and generate security reports. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the operator pods and ensuring reports are being generated.

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to access it.
